_source: TSU MSM Workshop May 2026, TSU Fast Track ADVISE, 4As Needs Analysis Model
_description: Discovery meeting questions using the 4As Needs Analysis Model and Marketing Strategy Model for TSI sellers

framework_overview.name: 4As Needs Analysis Model (CSS Sales Accelerator)
framework_overview.purpose: Uncover the prospect's real business challenges and desired outcomes so you can tailor a proposal to what matters most to them.

steps.purpose: Discover desired business results. Don't accept the first answer — uncover several to ensure a meaningful assignment.
steps.guidance: Ask questions that reveal what the business owner pays attention to on their P&L, what they're proud of, and what keeps them up at night.

steps.purpose: Summarize all the desired business results you've uncovered and select one to focus on.
steps.guidance: Don't pick for them. Surface the list, ask them to prioritize, then agree on the 1-2 that are most important and most addressable.

steps.purpose: Find out what's getting in the way of achieving the desired business result. Lead with the consumer journey.
steps.guidance: Dig deeper into the problem. What makes it tough to achieve? What consumer behavior needs to change? What has prevented them from solving it already?

steps.purpose: Agree on 2-3 ways they will measure the success of the campaign.
steps.guidance: Ask how they'll know if it's working. Push for specific, measurable success signals — not just 'more calls' but 'X more calls per week.'

a1_assessment_questions: Help me see this business through your eyes — what do you pay most attention to on your P&L?
You've built something here — what areas of the business are showing real promise right now?
What results are you most focused on achieving this year?
Which results are giving you the most trouble?
What opportunities are you trying to capitalize on in the next 6-12 months?
If you could change one thing about how this business performs, what would it be?
What does a great month look like for you versus a tough one?
Where does your new business come from today — and is that working as well as you'd like?
a2_assignment_questions: Based on everything you've shared, which of these is the most important to address right now?
If we could only focus on one of these, which would have the biggest impact on your business?
Is there anything missing from what we've talked about — anything that's really pressing that we haven't hit yet?
If we both invested our time in solving [specific result], would that feel like a meaningful win for you?
a3_analysis_questions: What makes it tough to achieve this result right now?
Who is the target customer you're trying to attract — can you describe who your best customer is?
Where do your customers typically come from today? What's their journey before they call or walk in?
Describe the path a typical new customer takes before they choose you.
What do you think gets in the way of more people like that finding you?
Who else are they considering? What does that competitive landscape look like?
What are the 2-3 things you'd need to see in an ideal solution to feel confident it would work?
Have you tried to solve this before? What happened?
a4_agreement_questions: How would you describe the ultimate measure of success for this kind of campaign?
What return on your investment are you looking for?
If a campaign ran for 90 days and you were evaluating it — what would you be looking at to decide if it worked?
Given what we've talked about around the consumer journey, how might we measure progress along the way?
What would be an early indicator that something is working — before we even get to 90 days?

msm_discovery_questions._description: Marketing Strategy Model questions — used during deeper discovery to understand targeting, competitive position, and messaging

step_1_targeting.focus: WHO the advertiser wants to market to
questions: Who is the ideal prospect for your products or services?
Who is your heavy user — the customer who comes back most often or spends the most?
Do you have different types of customers you're trying to reach? If so, who are the top two or three?
Describe your best customer in terms of age, income, lifestyle, or how they find you.

step_2_benefits_sought.focus: WHAT the target consumer really wants
questions: What does your customer really want when they choose you over a competitor?
What's the real benefit they're buying — not just the service, but the feeling or outcome?
What problem are they trying to solve when they come to you?
What do your best customers say about why they chose you and why they stay?

step_3_competitive_perceptions.focus: WHO ELSE offers what the consumer wants
questions: Who are your main competitors in [market]?
What do you think customers see as the difference between you and your competition?
If a customer is deciding between you and [competitor], what tips the decision in your favor?
Is there a competitor who you feel is doing a particularly good job of marketing right now?

step_4_positioning.focus: WHAT the advertiser promises to deliver
questions: What is your single biggest competitive advantage — the one thing you'd want every potential customer to know?
How would you describe your business in one or two sentences to someone who's never heard of you?
What do you want people to think of when they hear your name?
Is there a story or message about your business that you feel isn't getting out enough?

step_5_advertising_and_promotion.focus: The CAMPAIGN — why buy and why buy now
questions: Is there something timely or seasonal about your business that creates urgency?
Are you running any promotions or offers right now that should be part of the message?
What do you want a new customer to do after they see your advertising — call, visit, book online?
Have you run advertising before? What worked, and what didn't?

pain_and_dig_deeper_questions: Tell me more about that — how long has that been an issue?
What does that cost you when it doesn't work the way you want?
If you solved that, what would be different about the business 12 months from now?
How are you handling that challenge today? Is that working well?
What have you already tried to address it?
On a scale of 1-10, how important is solving this in the next 6 months?

components: Review the agenda — set a clear structure for the meeting
Align expectations — explain what you'll cover and what a successful meeting looks like
Transition to questioning — use the 4As model to drive the discovery
step_0_strong_opener.sample_opener: Thanks for making time — I want to be respectful of it. Here's what I'd love to do today: I'll ask you a few questions about the business and what you're trying to accomplish, and then I want to share a couple of ideas I think could be relevant for you. Sound good? Great — let me start here...

components: Summarize the key desired business results you heard
Set next steps — when and what the follow-up meeting will include
step_6_agreement.sample_close: Based on everything we talked about today, it sounds like the most important thing is [assignment]. And the way we'd know if we're winning is [measures of success]. Does that accurately reflect what matters most to you? Great — here's what I'd like to do: I'll come back on [date] with a specific proposal built around exactly that. Sound good?
